channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CISF Assigned to Ensure Security of All Indian Sea Ports
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Friday announced that the Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) will now be responsible for overseeing the security of sea ports throughout India. This was revealed...

item.title: Trump Prioritizes Iran Military Operation Over Rising Gas Prices
item.description: President Donald Trump told Reuters on Thursday that he is not worried about the rising gas prices in the U.S. which are driven by the widening conflict with Iran. He said the military operation is mo...

item.title: KUTRRH Releases Deceased Patient's Body After Intervention Over Unpaid Bill
item.description: In Nairobi, Kenyatta University Teaching, Referral & Research Hospital (KUTRRH) has finally released the body of a patient who passed away in November 2025, after it had been withheld due to an unpaid...

item.title: Heatherwick Studio Proposes Curved Residential Towers for Seoul’s Daegyo Apartments
item.description: Heatherwick Studio has unveiled an innovative plan to redevelop the Daegyo Apartments in Yeouido, Seoul. The project aims to replace the aging 1975-era complex with four new residential towers featuri...

item.title: Key Events and News in Ottawa for March 6–8
item.description: This weekend in Ottawa features a variety of local events and notable news stories. Residents can expect spring-like weather, providing an opportunity to enjoy outdoor activities. The city is seeing s...

item.title: Kenya Assures Safety of Its Nationals in Middle East as Conflict Continues
item.description: The Kenyan government has reassured the public that over 500,000 Kenyans living and working in the Middle East remain safe despite ongoing regional tensions. Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi s...

item.title: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor keeps City of London Freedom honour despite controversy
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will retain the Freedom of the City of London honour, following a review which concluded it cannot be revoked. The former prince, who was stripped of his royal titles due to...

item.title: Senegal Should Focus on Managing Short-Term Liquidity Rather Than Restructuring Debt
item.description: Even though Senegal's Finance Minister, Cheikh Diba, has rejected debt restructuring, there are increasing calls for it, with many seeing it as a technical necessity due to rising debt and higher debt...

item.title: Saint Paul University-Tuguegarao Graduate Tops February 2026 Nurses Licensure Exam
item.description: Alyanna Marie Ramos Villegas, a graduate of Saint Paul University - Tuguegarao, emerged as the top performer in the February 2026 Nurses Licensure Examination, according to the Professional Regulation...

item.title: Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics to begin amid international tensions and Russian flag controversy
item.description: The 2026 Winter Paralympics in Milan-Cortina are set to begin despite growing geopolitical tensions and controversy surrounding the display of the Russian flag. Several nations have announced boycotts...

item.title: Families Recall Losses and Isolation During Covid in UK Inquiry
item.description: The UK Covid inquiry, chaired by Baroness Hallett, has concluded its final evidence hearings, focusing on the personal experiences of families affected by the pandemic. Bereaved relatives shared emoti...

item.title: ANC faces internal tensions over Ekurhuleni mayor amid political disputes
item.description: Ekurhuleni mayor Nkosindiphile Xhakaza is under mounting pressure from within the ANC, as internal party divisions intensify over his leadership and controversial decisions. Key concerns include his a...

item.title: Menopause symptoms contribute to higher intentions to leave among secondary teachers in England
item.description: Secondary education in England is grappling with a severe recruitment and retention crisis, with over 90% of teachers departing before reaching retirement age. Government initiatives have primarily ta...

item.title: Pakistan's Development as the Only Muslim Nuclear Power Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The article discusses Pakistan’s emergence as the only Muslim-majority country with nuclear weapons, a status achieved largely in secrecy. It explores the historical context of Pakistan’s nuclear prog...

item.title: Women’s groups file complaint against QC Congressman Bong Suntay over lewd comments on Anne Curtis
item.description: Quezon City 4th District Representative Bong Suntay is facing a complaint before the Office of the Ombudsman after making lewd remarks about actress Anne Curtis. Women's rights groups, including the W...

item.title: Billy Idol Discusses Overcoming Heroin Addiction and Life Lessons
item.description: Rock icon Billy Idol recently opened up about his struggles with drug addiction, explaining that during his battle with heroin, he briefly turned to crack as a way to stop using heroin. Idol shared th...

item.title: Tribute to Judith Amwayi, Kenya’s Legendary Hockey Forward
item.description: Judith Amwayi, one of Kenya’s most celebrated women’s hockey players, passed away on February 14, 2026, in Duchess, southern Alberta, Canada. Born into a sporting family, Amwayi started her hockey jou...

item.title: Sandiganbayan upholds fugitive status of former lawmaker Zaldy Co over flood control project case
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has reaffirmed its decision declaring former House appropriations panel chairperson Zaldy Co of Ako Bicol party-list as a fugitive from justice. The anti-graft court denied Co's moti...

item.title: UN Declares West Asia Crisis a Humanitarian Emergency Amid Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: On March 6, 2026, the conflict between Israel and Iran intensified with Israeli airstrikes targeting the capitals of Iran and Lebanon. The U.S. reportedly struck an Iranian drone carrier at sea, escal...

item.title: U.S. Military Presence in Gulf States Increases Regional Security Risks
item.description: Before recent U.S.-Iran tensions escalated, Gulf countries believed hosting American military bases would strengthen their security by deterring attacks. However, recent events have shown that these b...
